| **Latent Heat Treatment** | Source term in $H$ | Artificial inflation of $C_p$ | Iterative temperature correction |
| **Numerical Stability** | Excellent | Good (may oscillate near $T_s, T_L$) | Excellent |
| **Accuracy of Mushy Zone** | High | Moderate | High |
| **Implementation Complexity** | Moderate | Low (easy in commercial FEM) | High |
| **Used In** | Most commercial codes | Teaching examples, MAGMA, ProCAST | Research codes |

---

<!-- SECTION_3_END -->

<!-- SECTION_4_START -->
# 4. Structural Diagrams & Schematics

## 4.1 Mermaid Block Diagram: Solidification Simulation Workflow

The following diagram illustrates the **end-to-end workflow** of a typical solidification simulation project, from CAD input to validated casting.

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[Step1 CAD Geometry] --> B[Step2 Mesh Generation]
    B --> C[Step3 Material Property DB]
    C --> D[Step4 Boundary Conditions]
    D --> E[Step5 Solver FEM Engine]
    E --> F[Step6 Post Processor]
    F --> G{Niyama Defect Check}
    G -->|Niyama below threshold| H[Step7 Riser Redesign]
    H --> E
    G -->|Niyama above threshold| I[Step8 Report Generation]
    I --> J[Step9 Physical Trial Casting]
    J --> K{Validation OK}
    K -->|Yes| L[Step10 Production Release]
    K -->|No| M[Step11 Iterative Refinement]
    M --> A
```

## 4.2 Mermaid Block Diagram: Coupled Physics Solver Architecture

The following diagram maps the **interactions between the three coupled physics** inside a solidification simulation solver.

```mermaid

    A[Input Energy Equation] --> C[Coupled Solver Core]

    D[Input Continuity Equation] --> C
    E[Input Solid Fraction Model] --> C
    C --> F[Output Temperature Field]
    C --> G[Output Velocity Field]
    C --> H[Output Solid Fraction Map]
    C --> I[Output Shrinkage Porosity]
    C --> J[Output Hot Spot Locations]
```

The following shows a **typical industrial software stack** for casting simulation, mapping the functional layers used in real foundries (e.g., those using MAGMA, ProCAST, or FLOW-3D).
